note: Every program from faithandresults.com, one object each. Edit here — the app renders this file. 'trainings' lists ids from trainings.json that let people apply into the program.

pillars.name: Economic Development
pillars.blurb: Financial empowerment with the institutions that move money.

pillars.name: Education & Training
pillars.blurb: Workforce and business training designed and delivered nationally.

pillars.name: Sustainability
pillars.blurb: Congregations and organizations that outlast any single grant.

programs.name: Homes Saved By Faith
programs.body: FREEDOM's flagship: foreclosure prevention and intervention delivered through the congregations families already trust, built with the FDIC, HUD, and the Federal Home Loan Banks of Atlanta, Boston, and San Francisco — plus state and municipal housing finance authorities.
partners: Federal Home Loan Banks of Atlanta, Boston & San Francisco

programs.name: Freddie Mac Faith Empowerment Series
programs.body: A statewide financial-empowerment series delivered across Louisiana with Freddie Mac — bringing credit, homeownership, and household-finance education into faith communities from New Orleans outward.

programs.name: MLK Corridors Initiative
programs.body: Reimagining the streets that carry Dr. King's name — community-led design and development that turns symbolic corridors into engines of local economy.

programs.name: Workforce Readiness Program
programs.body: Training residents in the building trades and beyond — race and gender neutral by design — moving people into employment the economy actually hires for, through the congregations they already trust.

programs.name: NeighborWorks America Training Institutes
programs.body: FREEDOM designs and delivers training for NeighborWorks America — the country's largest HUD housing intermediary — including Training Institutes hosted in Connecticut.

programs.name: Capacity Building Training
programs.body: FREEDOM is the creator, sponsor, and trainer of faith-based organizations nationwide — governance, program design, and revenue that renews itself, so the mission outlasts any single grant.

programs.name: FREEDOM–Faith CDC Collaborative
programs.body: The housing development partnership: congregations and the Faith Community Development Corporation — the CDC chartered by Shiloh Baptist Church — building and preserving homes together, with finance authorities at the table from day one.

programs.name: Homebuyer Education & Counseling
programs.body: Homebuyer education, housing counseling, and financial literacy that walk families from renting to owning — delivered city by city with trusted local partners.

programs.name: Foreclosure Intervention Initiative — Cleveland
programs.body: Cleveland's collaboration in action: homebuyer education and counseling, financial literacy, and foreclosure intervention and prevention support, delivered with HomeFree USA and the Home Ownership Training Center.

programs.name: Congregational Sustainability & Solar
programs.body: Sustainability preached and practiced: under Rev. McCluster, Shiloh Baptist became the first African-American church in Connecticut to reach energy self-sufficiency through solar. FREEDOM brings that playbook — energy, capacity, governance — to congregations nationwide.

programs.name: Saving America's Soul
programs.body: The NFLC's national initiative: senior pastors across the country organized as one voice for community renewal — the moral case and the economic case, made together.
